subject: What's equivalent of RadPageView in WPF ?
body:
From: Martin
Date: 5/5/2015 1:46:43 AM

Hi,

The UI for WPF suite doesn't provide a control that is equivalent to the WinForms' RadPageView. Actually, we have components that cover the pageview's functionality but divided into several controls. Please take a look at the following controls and let me know if they work for you:

  • RadTabControl
  • RadPanelBar
  • RadOutlookBar

The only view that is not supported as a stand alone control is the Backstage view. We have such control, but it is a part of RadRibbonView and it is used along with it.

Regards,
Martin
Telerik

subject: Extending Visual Studio 2015, VS SDK NuGet and the CPS
body:
At Build 2015 we announced that Visual Studio will now be extensible as a single VS SDK NuGet package starting with VS2015 RTM. This means you can use NuGet to add the VS SDK content to your project and develop your extension using the common project system (CPS) which is the new project system for VS.

To get started, check out the Channel 9 video where Mads Kristensen walks through the new extension system.

subject: Intel beefs up fifth-gen Core chips with IRIS graphics
body:
Intel has announced an update to its fifth-generation Broadwell Core processor line, adding Iris 6100 graphics to give laptops a bigger GPU boost. The new chips, Core i5-5350U and Core i7-5557U, bring Intel's best integrated graphics yet to the 15W processor family favored by thin-and-light notebooks.

The 5th-Gen Core U lineup now spans from the basic HD 6000 graphics all the way to Iris 6100, which is about 40 percent faster than the previous best, Iris 5100. For those plugging into an external display or gaming at lower settings, that's a meaningful improvement.

subject: Visual Studio - Debugging Tips and Tricks
body:
Did you know that Visual Studio has many powerful features that can dramatically speed up your debugging workflow? In this post I wanted to share some of my favorite tips and tricks.

Conditional Breakpoints

Right-click a breakpoint and choose 'Conditions...' to set a condition that must be true for the breakpoint to fire. This is invaluable when debugging loops or searching for a specific case.

Data Tips

Hover over a variable during debugging to see its value, and pin data tips to keep them visible as you step through code.

Edit and Continue

Make changes to code while stopped at a breakpoint and continue execution without restarting the debug session.

subject: David De Gea torn between repaying United's faith and return home
body:
David De Gea finds himself in an extraordinarily difficult position as the summer transfer window approaches. The Spanish goalkeeper, who has been named Manchester United's player of the season for consecutive years, is understood to be torn between loyalty to the club that took a chance on an unproven 20-year-old and an emotional desire to return to his hometown club.

Real Madrid has made De Gea their priority transfer target this summer, and sources close to the player suggest the appeal of playing for his boyhood club is strong. Yet United manager Louis van Gaal has made it clear that De Gea is central to his plans.

subject: Luke Wood plays starring role for Nottinghamshire
body:
Luke Wood produced a performance of exceptional promise as Nottinghamshire defeated Yorkshire in a tense Royal London One-Day Cup match at Headingley yesterday. The 19-year-old left-arm seamer took four wickets for 29 runs and earned the Man of the Match award as his side won by 24 runs.

It was only Wood's third senior appearance but he showed a control and maturity belying his age. His ability to move the ball both ways and execute his yorker at will has already made him one of the most talked-about young pace bowlers in county cricket.

subject: Rafael Nadal & Novak Djokovic to meet at French Open
body:
Rafael Nadal and Novak Djokovic are set up for a blockbuster French Open semi-final after both powered through their respective quarter-final ties at Roland Garros on Wednesday.

Nadal, bidding for a record ninth title at his favourite Grand Slam, dispatched Tomas Berdych 6-3, 7-5, 6-1 in under two hours. Djokovic overcame a tricky first set against Andy Murray before winning 6-3, 6-3, 5-7, 5-7, 6-1 in what proved to be a fascinating encounter.

subject: Two World War I Soldiers to Posthumously Receive Medal of Honor
body:
The White House announced Tuesday that President Obama will award the Medal of Honor to two World War I soldiers who were previously overlooked due to their ethnicity. The decision follows a congressionally-mandated review to ensure minority soldiers were not unfairly denied recognition.

Henry Johnson, an African-American soldier who single-handedly fought off a German raiding party in 1918, and Jose de la Luz Saenz, a Mexican-American soldier who kept a diary of his service, will each receive the nation's highest military honour in a ceremony next month.

subject: Expecting Double click for grid values
body:
From: John
Date: 5/15/2015 10:00:00 AM

Hi,

I would like to implement double-click editing on RadGridView cells. When a user double-clicks a cell, it should open an edit dialog. Is this supported natively or do I need to handle the mouse event manually?

From: TelerikSupport
Date: 5/15/2015 2:00:00 PM

Hi John,

You can handle the MouseDoubleClick event on the RadGridView and check if the clicked element is a GridViewCell. Here's a quick example:

gridView.MouseDoubleClick += (s, e) => {
    var cell = e.OriginalSource as GridViewCell;
    if (cell != null) OpenEditDialog(cell.DataContext);
};

Regards,
Telerik Support

subject: Telerik RadGridView TabIndex Issue
body:
From: Alex
Date: 5/20/2015 9:30:00 AM

Hello,

I have a RadGridView on a form with several other controls. When I press Tab, the focus should move through the grid cells before moving to the next control. However, the TabIndex is not working as expected — focus jumps directly from the grid to the next control without navigating through cells.

From: TelerikSupport

Hi Alex,

The standard TabIndex property controls focus order between controls, but within RadGridView, Tab key navigation through cells is controlled by the NavigationStyle property. Set it to CellLevel to allow Tab to navigate through cells:

gridView.NavigationStyle = GridViewNavigationStyle.CellLevel;

Regards,
Telerik Support

subject: How can I implement "FilteringBehavior" ?
body:
From: Peter
Date: 5/22/2015 11:00:00 AM

Hi,

I need to implement custom filtering behavior for RadGridView. I want to filter not just by exact match but by a custom expression. What's the recommended approach?

From: TelerikSupport

Hi Peter,

You can create a custom FilterDescriptor by inheriting from FilterDescriptorBase and overriding the MatchesItem method. Alternatively, you can use the IFilterDescriptor interface.

Here is a simple example of a custom filter:

public class MyFilter : FilterDescriptorBase {
    protected override bool MatchesItem(object item) {
        var email = item as Email;
        return email?.Subject.Contains("Telerik") == true;
    }
}

Regards,
Telerik Support

subject: Performance Excel Export
body:
From: Lisa
Date: 5/25/2015 3:00:00 PM

Hello,

I'm using the RadGridView Excel export feature. When exporting 50,000 rows, it takes over 30 seconds. Is there a way to speed this up?

From: TelerikSupport

Hi Lisa,

For large exports, we recommend using the ExportToXlsx method with the async overload and setting ShowLoadingIndicator = true. Also, turning off AutoGenerateColumns and only exporting the columns you need will help significantly.

Another approach is to use our RadSpreadProcessing library directly, which gives you full control over the export process and is optimized for large datasets.

Regards,
Telerik Support

subject: RadGridView, billions of rows and loading on-the-fly
body:
From: Tom
Date: 5/28/2015 10:00:00 AM

Hi,

We have a database with billions of records and need to display them in RadGridView. We cannot load all data at once. What is the best approach for virtualized, on-demand loading?

From: TelerikSupport

Hi Tom,

RadGridView supports Virtual Mode which enables on-demand row loading. You handle the ItemsLoading event and provide items as needed.

Alternatively, we recommend wrapping your query in a QueryableCollectionView or using an IEnumerable with deferred execution. The grid will only request items visible in the viewport.

For maximum performance with large datasets, also consider enabling Row Virtualization and Column Virtualization.

Regards,
Telerik Support
